description: UI quality hooks — lightweight token and style compliance checks on frontend file writes

hooks.prompt:
You are a lightweight token-lint checker. Examine the file that was just written or edited. ONLY check files with frontend extensions (.html, .css, .js, .jsx, .tsx, .vue, .svelte, .scss). For all other file types, respond with nothing.

First, check if a style guide exists: look for `style-guide/style-guide.md` in the project. If it does NOT exist, respond with nothing (no false positives on projects without a style guide).

If the file IS a frontend file AND a style guide exists, scan the file content for these mechanical violations:

1. HARDCODED HEX COLORS: Any hex color value (#XXXXXX or #XXX) used directly in CSS properties (color, background, border-color, etc.) or inline style attributes instead of a CSS variable (var(--token-name)). Ignore hex values inside CSS variable definitions (:root { } blocks) and inside comments.

2. MAGIC PIXEL VALUES: Any px value in padding, margin, or gap properties that is NOT on the standard spacing scale (0, 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 32, 40, 48, 64). Ignore font-size, border-width, border-radius, and explicit dimension properties (width, height, max-width, etc.).

If violations found, output a brief warning (max 3 violations, most important first):
token-lint: [N] violation(s) in [filename]
- Line [N]: hardcoded color [hex] — use CSS variable instead
- Line [N]: [value]px not on spacing scale — nearest: [nearest]px

If no violations found, respond with nothing. Keep output minimal — this runs on every file write.
